svg.text: Avondale Membership Automation Overview
This note explains, in plain English, what the avondale-n8n processes do and how they support the
membership admin work of the club.
Terminology
n8n
: the workflow automation tool that runs the club's membership processes.
Metabase
: the reporting dashboard used to view signups, batches, exceptions, and trends.
What The Automation Is For
The automation exists to reduce manual membership admin after somebody joins or renews through ClubSpark.
In practice it does five main jobs:
1. captures new membership signups from ClubSpark emails
2. refreshes the latest member and contact data from ClubSpark
3. combines that information into one operational database
4. prepares batches for shoe tags, parent tags, keys, labels, and envelopes
5. keeps dashboards and reporting up to date
High-Level Functional Description
The process starts in ClubSpark. When someone buys a membership, ClubSpark sends a signup notification
email and also holds the latest member and contact records for that household.
Local n8n workflows regularly pick up those inputs in three ways:
• a parser reads the signup notification emails from Gmail
• an export workflow pulls the current members list from ClubSpark
• another export workflow pulls the current contacts list from ClubSpark
Those three inputs are brought together in the local PostgreSQL database, which is the club's operational
source of truth for this process. This is where the system works out who signed up, who is paying, what
address should be used, and what still needs to be processed.
From there, the workflows support the committee's day-to-day membership handling:
• new signups are grouped into a processing batch
• batch data is turned into printable address labels and DL envelopes
• members without a usable postal address can be handled by email instead
• manual items can be added when tags or keys are needed outside the normal signup flow
• missed signups can be backfilled if ClubSpark shows the membership but the original email was not captured
The same local data is then synced to the cloud reporting copy so that Metabase dashboards stay current.
This gives the committee visibility of signups, batches, exceptions, and history without making the cloud
system the live operational system.
